description: 8 WASHINGTON The AllenScott Report By ROBERT S ALLEN and PAUL SCOTT WASHINGTON Ambassador Henry Cabot Lodge is carrying tough Instructions to strifetorn South Vietnam despite those official denials to the contrary In efiect he will tell President Ngo Dinh Diem that unless the turbulent wrangling with the Buddhists is prompt d it will be publicly disavowed the US The clear inference of this blunt warning is that Washington i 3 prepared to officially reprimand Diem Such a crackdown wou ilmost certainly have far ing ernal impact in South Vietnan It could touch off upheaval that might mean the end of the longembattled Diem resime These stern instructions were personally given by the President to Lodge before he departed for Saigon The President expressed sharp impatience with Diems dis ruptive handling of the thorny Buddhist problem particularly tie provocative comments of Madame Ngo Dinh Nhu his outspoken sisterinlaw This trouble with the Buddhists is wholly unnecessary the President said There is no real justification for it Its as disruptive as it is incomprehensible Genuine progress was being made in the costly struggle against the Communists Now thats being seriously jeopardized by this needless internal dissension We have vital strategic interests
